prompt: Professional comparison matrix diagram. Two columns: left column labeled 'Microsoft Entra' with items marked red X for gaps (No autonomous agent OBO, No FGA, No MCP Adapter, No Token Vault, Azure-locked Managed Identities). Right column labeled 'Okta O4AA' with items marked green checkmarks (OBO Token Exchange GA, Auth0 FGA, MCP Adapter, Token Vault, Multi-cloud). Clean corporate style, blue header bar, white background, crisp table layout.

Professional comparison matrix diagram. Two columns: left column labeled 'AWS IAM' with items marked red X for gaps (No user delegation in Bedrock, Single IAM role per agent, CloudTrail shows role not user, AWS-only scope, No RFC 8693). Right column labeled 'Okta O4AA' with items marked green checkmarks (OBO per-user delegation, Per-agent identity, User-attributed audit, Multi-cloud, RFC 8693 GA). Clean corporate style, blue header, white background.

Professional comparison matrix diagram. Two columns: left column labeled 'Ping Identity for AI' with items marked orange partial (Runtime Identity new GA, PingFederate OBO requires expert config, PingAuthorize needs integration, No Token Vault, PingOne and PingFederate fragmented). Right column labeled 'Okta O4AA' with items marked green checkmarks (XAA plus OBO GA, Integrated FGA, Token Vault, MCP Adapter 8 methods, Single unified platform). Clean corporate style, blue header, white background.

Professional market landscape quadrant diagram. Four quadrants with axes: horizontal axis 'AI Agent Capability' from Low to High, vertical axis 'Enterprise Readiness' from Low to High. Top-right quadrant (high capability, high enterprise): Okta star icon. Top-left (low capability, high enterprise): Microsoft Entra dot. Bottom-right (high capability, low enterprise): Ping dot. Bottom-left: CyberArk dot, SailPoint dot, AWS dot. Clean corporate style, blue and white palette, professional chart aesthetic.
